datasets.name: ds_otp_donut
datasets.displayName: OTP Summary ã Early / On-Time / Late
datasets.query:
-- Swiftly window: 1 min early (-60s) / 6 min late (360s)
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN arrival_delay_seconds < -60  THEN 'Early'
    WHEN arrival_delay_seconds <= 360  THEN 'On-Time'
    ELSE 'Late'
  END                                                          AS otp_category,
  COUNT(*)                                                     AS stop_count,
  ROUND(COUNT(*) * 100.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 1)          AS pct
FROM tabular.dataexpert.gold_stop_dwell_fact
WHERE service_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND actual_arrival_ts IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY otp_category
ORDER BY otp_category

datasets.name: ds_otp_trend
datasets.displayName: OTP Trend ã Daily Early / On-Time / Late (30 days)
datasets.query:
SELECT
  service_date,
  COUNT(*)                                                     AS total_stops,
  ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN arrival_delay_seconds < -60   THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) * 100, 1) AS early_pct,
  ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN arrival_delay_seconds BETWEEN -60 AND 360 THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) * 100, 1) AS on_time_pct,
  ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN arrival_delay_seconds > 360   THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) * 100, 1) AS late_pct
FROM tabular.dataexpert.gold_stop_dwell_fact
WHERE service_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 30 DAYS
  AND actual_arrival_ts IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY service_date
ORDER BY service_date

datasets.name: ds_stop_otp
datasets.query:
SELECT
  stop_id,
  route_id,
  direction_id,
  MIN(stop_sequence)                                           AS stop_order,
  COUNT(*)                                                     AS observations,
  ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN arrival_delay_seconds < -60   THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) * 100, 1) AS early_pct,
  ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN arrival_delay_seconds BETWEEN -60 AND 360 THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) * 100, 1) AS on_time_pct,
  ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN arrival_delay_seconds > 360   THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) * 100, 1) AS late_pct,
  ROUND(AVG(arrival_delay_seconds) / 60.0, 1)                 AS avg_delay_min,
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_APPROX(arrival_delay_seconds, 0.5) / 60.0, 1) AS p50_delay_min,
  ROUND(PERCENTILE_APPROX(arrival_delay_seconds, 0.9) / 60.0, 1) AS p90_delay_min
FROM tabular.dataexpert.gold_stop_dwell_fact
WHERE service_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND actual_arrival_ts IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY stop_id, route_id, direction_id
HAVING observations >= 5
ORDER BY route_id, direction_id, stop_order

datasets.name: ds_tod_otp
datasets.displayName: OTP by Time of Day ã 15-min buckets (last 7 days)
datasets.query:
SELECT
  -- Floor to 15-min bucket in Phoenix local time
  DATE_FORMAT(
    TIMESTAMPADD(
      MINUTE,
      FLOOR(MINUTE(TIMESTAMPADD(HOUR, -7, actual_arrival_ts)) / 15) * 15
        - MINUTE(TIMESTAMPADD(HOUR, -7, actual_arrival_ts)),
      TIMESTAMPADD(HOUR, -7, actual_arrival_ts)
    ),
    'HH:mm'
  )                                                            AS time_bucket_phoenix,
  SUM(CASE WHEN arrival_delay_seconds < -60   THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS early_count,
  SUM(CASE WHEN arrival_delay_seconds BETWEEN -60 AND 360 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS on_time_count,
  SUM(CASE WHEN arrival_delay_seconds > 360   THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS late_count,
  COUNT(*)                                                     AS total_count
FROM tabular.dataexpert.gold_stop_dwell_fact
WHERE service_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND actual_arrival_ts IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY time_bucket_phoenix
ORDER BY time_bucket_phoenix

datasets.name: ds_delay_histogram
datasets.query:
SELECT
  ROUND(arrival_delay_seconds / 60.0)                          AS delay_minute,
  CASE
    WHEN arrival_delay_seconds < -60  THEN 'Early'
    WHEN arrival_delay_seconds <= 360 THEN 'On-Time'
    ELSE 'Late'
  END                                                          AS otp_category,
  COUNT(*)                                                     AS stop_count
FROM tabular.dataexpert.gold_stop_dwell_fact
WHERE service_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 7 DAYS
  AND actual_arrival_ts IS NOT NULL
  AND arrival_delay_seconds BETWEEN -1200 AND 3600
GROUP BY delay_minute, otp_category
ORDER BY delay_minute

datasets.name: ds_schedule
datasets.displayName: Schedule View ã Trip x Stop delays (yesterday)
datasets.query:
SELECT
  t.service_date,
  t.route_id,
  t.direction_id,
  t.trip_id,
  -- Planned departure of first stop = trip start time
  MIN(t.scheduled_arrival_ts) OVER (PARTITION BY t.trip_id)   AS trip_start_ts,
  t.stop_sequence,
  t.stop_id,
  ROUND(t.arrival_delay_seconds / 60.0, 1)                    AS delay_min,
  CASE
    WHEN t.arrival_delay_seconds < -60  THEN 'early'
    WHEN t.arrival_delay_seconds <= 360 THEN 'on_time'
    ELSE 'late'
  END                                                          AS otp_category,
  t.scheduled_arrival_ts,
  t.actual_arrival_ts
FROM tabular.dataexpert.gold_stop_dwell_fact t
WHERE t.service_date = CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 1 DAYS
  AND t.actual_arrival_ts IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY t.route_id, t.direction_id, trip_start_ts, t.stop_sequence

widget.name: txt_summary
widget.textbox_spec:
## OTP Summary

Per-stop OTP using Swiftly window: **1 min early (-60s) / 6 min late (+360s)**. Filters: Route + Direction (left sidebar).

**Suggested visualizations:**
- `ds_otp_donut` ã Pie/Donut: dimension=`otp_category`, measure=`stop_count` ã mirrors Swiftly summary donut
- `ds_otp_donut` ã Table: shows Early / On-Time / Late counts and %
- `ds_otp_trend` ã Line chart: x=`service_date`, y=`early_pct` + `on_time_pct` + `late_pct` (3 series) ã mirrors Swiftly OTP over time
- `ds_otp_trend` ã Table: daily breakdown

widget.name: txt_stops
widget.textbox_spec:
## OTP by Stop

Per-stop Early/On-Time/Late breakdown. Sorted by `stop_order` (stop_sequence) to match route geography ã first stop at top, last stop at bottom.

**Suggested visualizations:**
- `ds_stop_otp` ã Bar chart: x=`stop_id`, y=`early_pct` + `on_time_pct` + `late_pct` as stacked series, sorted by `stop_order` ã mirrors Swiftly Stops tab
- `ds_stop_otp` ã Table: all columns for drill-down (p50/p90 delay per stop not in Swiftly but useful)

**Note:** Apply Route + Direction global filters to see a single route in stop order.

widget.name: txt_tod
widget.textbox_spec:
## OTP by Time of Day

15-minute buckets in Phoenix local time (UTC-7). Y axis = number of stop departures. Mirrors Swiftly Time of Day tab.

**Suggested visualizations:**
- `ds_tod_otp` ã Stacked bar: x=`time_bucket_phoenix`, y=`early_count` + `on_time_count` + `late_count` as stacked series ã exact Swiftly match
- Set colors: Early=pink (#e57373), On-Time=teal (#4db6ac), Late=yellow (#ffb74d)
- `ds_tod_otp` ã Table: shows raw counts per bucket

widget.name: txt_histogram
widget.textbox_spec:
## Delay Distribution Histogram

Distribution of per-stop delays in 1-minute bins. X = minutes early (negative) or late (positive). Mirrors Swiftly Histogram tab.

**Suggested visualizations:**
- `ds_delay_histogram` ã Bar chart: x=`delay_minute`, y=`stop_count`, series=`otp_category` (stacked)
- Set colors: Early=pink, On-Time=teal, Late=yellow
- X axis range: -20 to +60 covers 99%+ of observations
- `ds_delay_histogram` ã Table: raw counts by minute bucket

widget.name: txt_schedule
widget.textbox_spec:
## Schedule View ã Trip û Stop Delays

Shows yesterday's data. Each row = one stop observation with delay in minutes. Filter by Route + Direction to see a specific run. Sorted by trip start time then stop sequence.

**Note:** Swiftly's Schedule tab is a pivot matrix (trips as rows, stops as columns). Databricks tables can't pivot natively ã use this as a sortable/filterable table instead. The frontend playback UI will render the full matrix view.

**Suggested visualization:**
- `ds_schedule` ã Table: columns = `trip_start_ts`, `trip_id`, `stop_sequence`, `stop_id`, `delay_min`, `otp_category`
- Sort by `trip_start_ts` ASC then `stop_sequence` ASC
- Color-code `otp_category`: early=pink, on_time=teal, late=yellow (use conditional formatting if available)
